sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Borussia Dortmund are in solid form, remaining unbeaten in their last 3 matches with 2 wins and a draw. Borussia M'gladbach have had a mixed run, managing 2 wins and a draw in their last 5 games, but suffering losses in their most recent home fixtures.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Dortmund hold a strong advantage in recent head-to-head encounters, winning 4 of the last 5 matchups against M'gladbach. The last two meetings produced goals on both sides, with Dortmund securing a 3-2 victory in April 2025 and a 1-1 draw in December 2024.

sections.subtitle: Key Players
sections.text: Serhou Guirassy stands out for Dortmund with 4 goals in recent matches against M'gladbach and an average Sofascore rating of 7.54. For M'gladbach, Haris Tabaković has been a threat with 7 goals this season, ranking 4th in the league.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Dortmund, sitting 3rd with 29 points, have a robust defense (12 goals conceded, 2nd best in the league) and convert big chances at a high rate (50%, 3rd in Bundesliga). M'gladbach, 11th with 16 points, struggle defensively (22 goals conceded) and have less attacking efficiency.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Dortmund’s ability to dominate final third entries (average 64 per game) and create big chances (32 this season) could exploit M'gladbach’s defensive vulnerabilities. M'gladbach will likely rely on late goals (27% in the 76-90 minute period) to stay competitive.

sections.subtitle: Prediction
sections.text: Given Dortmund’s superior form, home advantage at Signal Iduna Park, and historical edge, they are favored to win. Sofascore’s prediction of a 61% chance for a home win aligns with the data, though M'gladbach’s scoring ability suggests a close contest.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, Borussia Dortmund lead Borussia M'gladbach 1-0 at Signal Iduna Park. The home side struck early through Julian Brandt in the 10th minute, capitalizing on their dominance in possession and territory. Dortmund have controlled much of the game, showing greater intent in the final third, while M'gladbach have struggled to create meaningful chances. The first half has been a story of Dortmund's superior ball control and aerial dominance, contrasted by M'gladbach's inability to convert limited opportunities into real threats.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Home team performance
halftimeSections.text: Borussia Dortmund have been the more assertive side in this encounter. They've dominated possession and made numerous entries into the final third and penalty area, showcasing their attacking intent. Their defense has been solid, particularly in aerial duels, where they’ve consistently outperformed their opponents. Key contributions from players like Julian Brandt, who scored the opener, and Niklas Süle, who provided an assist, have given them the edge. Despite missing a big chance, Dortmund have looked dangerous and likely to add to their tally if they maintain this level of intensity.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Away team performance
halftimeSections.text: Borussia M'gladbach have had a tough first half, failing to register a significant threat on goal. Their entries into the final third have been limited, and they’ve struggled to win aerial battles, which has hampered their ability to build sustained pressure. While they've managed to win some possession in dangerous areas, they lack the cutting edge needed to break through Dortmund’s defense. Without a notable shift in approach, M'gladbach face an uphill battle to get back into this game.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The game started with a brief spell of pressure from the away side, as M'gladbach tried to assert themselves early on. However, momentum quickly shifted to Dortmund, who took control with a series of attacking waves leading up to their goal. Following the opener, the home team maintained their grip on the game with consistent forward surges. While M'gladbach showed flickers of intent in isolated moments, particularly towards the end of the half, they couldn’t match Dortmund’s sustained attacking rhythm for most of the period.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Dortmund, Julian Brandt remains a pivotal figure after his first-half goal, showing sharpness in attack. Serhou Guirassy also poses a threat, given his involvement in creating chances and high expected assists. On the M'gladbach side, Giovanni Reyna could be key to unlocking Dortmund’s defense, as he’s shown creativity despite limited opportunities. Florian Neuhaus might also step up if given space to exploit in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Dortmund are well-positioned to extend their lead if they continue to dominate possession and capitalize on their chances. Their ability to create big opportunities in the first half suggests they could be more clinical after the break. M'gladbach, on the other hand, will need to take risks to equalize, potentially leaving gaps at the back. A more aggressive approach from the visitors might open up the game, but it could also play into Dortmund’s hands. Expect the home side to push for at least one more goal while M'gladbach search for a way back into the contest.
